response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a skilled full-stack engineer with genuine geospatial project experience via GISCollective and solid distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic, but his primary technical stack is JavaScript/TypeScript and Node.js, which does not align with the role's core requirement of modern C++ or Rust. His C++ work is limited to a six-month engagement at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, both over a decade old and neither at the depth expected for a senior individual contributor role. There is no Rust experience, no routing or navigation pipeline work, and no tile-based geospatial data processing in his background, making him a poor fit for this position as defined.
strengths: Genuine geospatial domain interest and hands-on project experience through GISCollective, including a FOSS4G 2019 conference presentation
Demonstrated ability to build and operate high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and a track record of cross-stack adaptability across many languages and environments
concerns: Primary language stack is JavaScript/TypeScript; C++ experience is minimal, dated (2014–2015), and not at the depth required for a senior C++ role
No Rust experience mentioned anywhere in the resume or project portfolio
No experience with routing tiles, navigation data pipelines, map tile optimization, or any production routing/navigation guidance system

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
